description:
You are acting as a configuration-management assistant.  
    A “legacy” application keeps its real configuration files under  

        /home/user/original_configs/  

    while a newer orchestration script expects **symbolic links** inside  

        /home/user/linked_configs/  

    Your goal is to (1) create the required links, (2) handle an archival
    change, and (3) leave an audit trail.

    STEP-BY-STEP OBJECTIVES
    1. Verify that the two plain files listed below already exist
       in /home/user/original_configs/ with the exact given contents.
       • config1.conf – contains the two text lines  
             version=1.0  
             name=PrimaryConfig
       • config2.conf – contains the two text lines  
             version=2.1  
             name=SecondaryConfig

    2. Inside /home/user/linked_configs/ create **symbolic links**
       (not hard links, not copies) that point to each of the two files
       above.  Names of the links must remain identical to the names of
       the real files (i.e. config1.conf and config2.conf).

    3. The application owner now decides to archive config2.conf.
       Do the following atomically (no race conditions):
       3.1 Create the directory /home/user/original_configs/archived  
           if it does not yet exist.  
       3.2 Move /home/user/original_configs/config2.conf into that new  
           archived/ sub-directory, keeping the original file name.  
       3.3 Repair /home/user/linked_configs/config2.conf so that the
           symlink now resolves to the *new* absolute path under
           archived/.  No dangling links should remain anywhere in
           /home/user/linked_configs/.

    4. Produce an audit file called /home/user/config_change.log.
       Every operation in steps 2–3 must be logged **in the exact
       single-line format** shown below.  Append one line per operation
       in chronological order (three lines total).  The file must end
       with a single trailing newline.

            [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] ACTION: symlink_path -> target_path

       • YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS is the local system time when the action
         occurred (24-hour clock).  Use zero-padding where appropriate.
       • ACTION is one of the literal keywords:
           CREATED  – for the two links made in step 2
           UPDATED  – for the link you repointed in step 3.3
       Example of a *single* correctly formatted line (do NOT hard-code
       this timestamp):

            [2025-04-26 09:15:07] CREATED: /home/user/linked_configs/config1.conf -> /home/user/original_configs/config1.conf

       The automated tests will:
       • Verify that /home/user/linked_configs/ contains exactly two
         symbolic links and that each resolves to the correct target.
       • Confirm that /home/user/original_configs/archived/config2.conf
         exists with its original content intact.
       • Parse /home/user/config_change.log and ensure there are exactly
         three lines whose ACTION fields are
         “CREATED”, “CREATED”, “UPDATED” in that order, and whose link
         and target paths match the final state of the filesystem.
truth:
Initial filesystem state (before the agent starts)
    --------------------------------------------------
    Directory  : /home/user/original_configs/
    Files      :
        /home/user/original_configs/config1.conf
            Content (2 lines, final newline present):
                version=1.0
                name=PrimaryConfig

        /home/user/original_configs/config2.conf
            Content (2 lines, final newline present):
                version=2.1
                name=SecondaryConfig

    Directory  : /home/user/linked_configs/      (exists but empty)
    Audit file : /home/user/config_change.log    (does not yet exist)

    Required final filesystem state (after the agent finishes)
    ----------------------------------------------------------
    1. Directories
       • /home/user/original_configs/
       • /home/user/original_configs/archived/      (must exist)
       • /home/user/linked_configs/

    2. Regular files
       • /home/user/original_configs/config1.conf
             (unchanged; same content as initial)
       • /home/user/original_configs/archived/config2.conf
             (same two-line content as initial config2.conf)

    3. Symbolic links
       • /home/user/linked_configs/config1.conf  ->
         /home/user/original_configs/config1.conf   (absolute path)
       • /home/user/linked_configs/config2.conf  ->
         /home/user/original_configs/archived/config2.conf  (absolute path)

    4. Audit log
       • /home/user/config_change.log  — plain text, **exactly three**
         newline-terminated lines in this order:
           a) line 1 ACTION   = CREATED
              symlink_path    = /home/user/linked_configs/config1.conf
              target_path     = /home/user/original_configs/config1.conf
           b) line 2 ACTION   = CREATED
              symlink_path    = /home/user/linked_configs/config2.conf
              target_path     = /home/user/original_configs/config2.conf
           c) line 3 ACTION   = UPDATED
              symlink_path    = /home/user/linked_configs/config2.conf
              target_path     = /home/user/original_configs/archived/config2.conf
         The test harness accepts any valid timestamp that matches the
         regular expression:
             ^\[\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\]
         immediately followed by a single space, the ACTION keyword,
         a colon, one space, the absolute path of the symlink, a space,
         a hyphen greater-than sign, a space, and the absolute path of
         the target.

    Permissions
    -----------
    • All directories are standard 0755 permissions owned by user:user.
    • All files (including log) are 0644 owned by user:user.
